title: Hailey Storage Tower
summary: A tall face-framed shelving tower with a door, sized to flank the Hailey storage bed — bookshelf up top, closed cabinet below, crown to match the bed.
description:
These towers stand at the head of the Hailey storage bed to make a built-in bedroom wall: open shelves up high, a closed door cabinet down low, a face frame for a furniture-grade front, and a bit of crown to tie it to the bed. Build one or a pair. On its own it's a handsome, sturdy bookcase-cabinet.

It starts, like most storage, as a plain 1x12 box — and because it's fully face-framed with a plywood back, you can build the box with just a nailer. The face frame is the piece that makes it look bought: add the long 1x2 side trim first, nailing into the fixed shelves for support, then the shelf trim. The 1/4" plywood back glues to all the fixed shelves and squares the tower.

The base uses 2x4 legs ripped to 3" wide to match the bed system; if you don't rip them, the 2x2 spacers need to be 1" shorter — the plan flags this, and it's the kind of detail that throws a base off if missed. Build doors to fit your actual openings with a 1/8" gap around them; overlay doors are more forgiving than inset if you're not confident. Because it's tall, anchor it to the wall to prevent tipping.
timeLabel: 6–9 hrs including a door and finishing

tools.note: For ripping the 2x4 legs to 3"; skip it and make the 2x2 spacers 1" shorter.
The face-framed box builds with a nailer.

cutList.note: 1" shorter if the legs aren't ripped to 3".

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Start by building a box out of the 1x12 sides and shelves. Because it's fully face-framed with a plywood back, you can build it with a nailer — just keep it square.

steps.title: Add the face frame
steps.body: Add the face frame, starting with the longer 1x2 side trim, using 1-1/4" finish nails and glue and nailing into the fixed shelves for support. Then add the shelf trim.

steps.title: Add the back
steps.body: Attach the 1/4" plywood to the back with glue, fastening to all the fixed shelves. The back squares and stiffens the tall tower.

steps.title: Build the base and crown
steps.body: Build the base from the ripped 2x4 legs, 2x2 spacers, and supports — if you didn't rip the legs to 3", make the 2x2 spacers 1" shorter. Add a bit of 1x3 crown up top to match the bed.

steps.title: Build a door, finish, and anchor
steps.body: Build the door to fit its actual opening with a 1/8" gap all around — overlay doors are more forgiving than inset if you're unsure. Hang it, sand, and finish. Because it's tall, anchor the tower to the wall to prevent tipping.

images.alt: Tall face-framed storage tower with shelves and a door
